cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out step by step. So the question is about Jane's birthday and figuring out a date a month ago from today's date. Let me start by breaking down the information given. First, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Now, the last day of February depends on whether it's a leap year. 2001 is not a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4, and 2001 isn't (2000 is, but 2001 isn't). So the last day of February 2001 would be February 28, 2001. So Jane's birthday is on 02/28/2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So we need to figure out what today's date is. If she was born in 2001, then her 16th birthday would be in 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today's date is February 28, 2017. Wait, but 2017 is not a leap year either. So again, the last day of February is the 28th. So today is 02/28/2017. Now the question is asking, what is the date a month ago from today? So if today is February 28, 2017, then a month ago would be January 28, 2017, right? Because going back one month from February 28 would take us to January 28. However, I should check if there's any nuance here. Sometimes when dealing with dates, if the day doesn't exist in the previous month, it adjusts. For example, if today was March 31, a month ago would be February 28 (or 29 if it's a leap year). But in this case, since we're going back from February 28 to January, which has 31 days, so January 28 exists. So the date a month ago would be 01/28/2017. Looking at the options, option D is 01/28/2017. So that should be the correct answer. Let me check the other options to make sure I didn't make a mistake. Option A is 11/12/2016. That's November 12, 2016. That's over a year ago, so not a month ago from today. Option B is 01/21/2017. That's 7 days earlier than January 28, so not correct. Option C is 01/14/2017, even earlier. Option E is 02/03/2017, which is 25 days after January 28, so that would be a month from now, not a month ago. Wait, but another way to think about a month ago is 30 days. Let me check the exact number of days. If today is February 28, 2017, then a month ago would be January 28, 2017. But if someone counts a month as 30 days, then 30 days before February 28 would be January 29 (since February has 28 days in 2017). But this is getting into the difference between calendar months and days. The standard way to subtract a month is to go back one month in the calendar, keeping the same day if possible.
